title: Dog Labor: 2 Hours of Contractions with No Puppy - Emergency Guide
meta_desc: Active labor for 2 hours without a puppy is a veterinary emergency. Learn the signs of dystocia and why you must contact a vet immediately.
content_html:
What Is Happening?

When a dog enters the second stage of labor, she begins active abdominal contractions to push the puppies through the birth canal. Under normal circumstances, a puppy should be delivered within 30 to 60 minutes of active straining. If your dog has been having strong contractions for two hours without producing a puppy, she is likely experiencing dystocia, which is the medical term for a difficult or obstructed birth.

Is It an Emergency?

Urgency Level: High. Yes, this is a critical medical emergency. If active, visible straining continues for more than 45 to 60 minutes without a puppy being born, or if your dog seems exhausted and has stopped straining after a long period of labor, the lives of both the mother and the puppies are at risk. You should contact an emergency veterinarian or your local clinic immediately for intervention, which may include medication to assist contractions or an emergency C-section.

Why a Photo or Video Helps Triage

If you are able to safely do so, taking a short video of the contractions or a clear photo of the vulva can be vital for the veterinary triage team. These visuals allow professionals to assess the strength and frequency of the contractions and check for any visible obstructions, such as a puppy stuck in the birth canal. Seeing the color of any discharge—specifically looking for dark green fluid before the first puppy arrives—helps the vet determine the level of fetal distress and prepare the surgical team before you even arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: How long can a dog go between puppies?
faqs.answer: While a rest period is normal, active straining should not last more than 60 minutes. If the mother is resting comfortably, the gap between puppies can occasionally be up to 2 hours, but any longer requires professional advice.

faqs.question: What does green discharge mean?
faqs.answer: Dark green discharge (uteroverdin) indicates that a placenta has detached. If this happens before a puppy is born, it is a sign of fetal distress and requires immediate veterinary attention.

faqs.question: Can I help pull a stuck puppy out?
faqs.answer: You should only attempt to assist if the puppy is clearly visible and you have received specific instructions from a veterinarian over the phone, as improper technique can cause severe internal damage to the mother or fatal injury to the puppy.
